plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate high-risk exposure.
Trim SPY to 20% and QQQI to 15% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall exposure.
Buy XLV (Healthcare) at 8% to diversify into a defensive sector with a strong scanner setup.
Buy RTX (Aerospace/Defense) at 7% to further diversify into a quality industrial name.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and reduces overall portfolio risk and leverage.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for a ~1.5%/month return, focusing on capital preservation and reduced volatility. Expect lower drawdowns but also more modest growth potential.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) as they are below stop and reduce overall leverage.
Trim SPY to 25% and QQQI to 10% to meet concentration limits and rebalance the portfolio.
Buy ANET (Networking) at 8% and CRDO (Connectivity) at 7% to add quality tech with strong risk/reward.
This plan balances growth with risk management, aiming for a diversified portfolio.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month, focusing on risk-first growth with moderate exposure. It aims for a blend of broad market exposure and quality tech, with controlled leverage.

plan: Sell SOXL and SPAL as they are below stop, reallocating capital to stronger setups.
Trim SPY to 25% and QQQI to 5% to meet concentration limits and free up capital for high-conviction plays.
Buy ANET at 12% and Add to CRDO at 10% for aggressive exposure to top-scoring tech setups.
Add to SMH at 18% to maintain semiconductor exposure with a strong current scanner read.
This plan aggressively leans into high-conviction setups and reallocates from underperforming assets.
aggressive.read: This aggressive growth plan targets ~4%/month, aiming for stretched growth by concentrating in high-scoring setups. Be aware that this approach significantly raises both upside and drawdown potential, and leveraged ETFs (if used) decay daily. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage losses.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and high-beta tech (SMH, CRWD) to minimize volatility.
Trim SPY to 20% to reduce broad market concentration.
Add to QQQI, increasing its allocation to 30% for enhanced monthly distributions.
This plan prioritizes steady income and low drawdown over growth, acting as a cash-like income strategy.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month, focusing on reliable monthly distributions and low drawdown. While prioritizing income, remember distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in selloffs. This is for cash-like income, not a savings account.
